9e78f29546ab.title: Starwatch: this week’s rare blue moon highlights the strange way we mark our calendar
9e78f29546ab.summary:
Because we define dates based on the stars, the full moon on 31 May will be the second one of the calendar month

This week’s full moon is a blue moon. The term does not describe the colour of the moon, but instead arises from the way we define our calendar in reference to the stars rather than the moon.

The moon takes almost a month to circle our planet. The exact time is 29.5 days but if we were to define our calendar as 12 lunar months, the year would fall short by around 11 day

0e74d88a2689.title: China launches three-crew spaceflight as part of lunar ambitions
0e74d88a2689.summary:
Mission will put first astronaut in orbit for a year, a key step in Beijing’s plan to put people on the moon by 2030

China has launched its Shenzhou-23 mission in which an astronaut will spend a full year in orbit for the first time, a crucial step in Beijing’s ambition to send humans to the moon by 2030.

The Long March 2-F rocket lifted off from the Jiuquan launch centre in north-western China on Sunday, carrying three astronauts to the Tiangong space station.

99c894d3a9ea.title: This prehistoric fish may explain how animals first walked on Earth
99c894d3a9ea.summary: Scientists have peered inside the skull of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish that was closely related to the first animals to walk on land, revealing surprising clues about how life began its move out of the water. Using advanced neutron imaging, researchers discovered that Koharalepis jarviki had features suited for living near the water’s surface, including openings in its skull that may have helped it gulp air and a light-sensitive organ linked to day-night rhythms.

be4f344404cb.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
be4f344404cb.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it only the fourth known example of such structures appearing in insects at all.

Scientists also found a possible way to extend the drugs’ effects, potentially helping patients push past weight-loss plateaus.

d1e1db6080c8.title: AI won’t replace you but someone using AI might
d1e1db6080c8.summary: Generative AI is transforming the workplace faster than ever, but new research from the University of Vaasa suggests the biggest threat may not be AI itself — it’s falling behind in learning how to use it. Researcher Zhe Zhu found that employees who see tools like ChatGPT and Gemini as helpful collaborators rather than job-stealing rivals tend to be more engaged, adaptable, and optimistic about their careers.

dcf2294cad87.title: Adorable tiny blue octopus found nearly 6,000 feet beneath the Galápagos
dcf2294cad87.summary: A mysterious little blue octopus discovered nearly 6,000 feet beneath the waters of the Galápagos Islands has officially been identified as a brand-new species. About the size of a golf ball, the tiny creature stunned researchers during a deep-sea expedition when it suddenly appeared on camera, crawling across the ocean floor near an underwater mountain.

13b38cba484e.title: Beet juice lowers blood pressure in older adults in just 2 weeks
13b38cba484e.summary: Drinking nitrate-rich beetroot juice may do more than support heart health — it could actually reshape the bacteria living in the mouth in ways that help lower blood pressure in older adults. In the largest study of its kind, researchers found that older people who drank concentrated beetroot juice twice daily for two weeks experienced noticeable blood pressure reductions, while younger adults did not.

a1a5e38df28d.title: Common heart drug taken by millions found useless — and possibly dangerous
a1a5e38df28d.summary: Новое масштабное исследование показало, что широко используемые бета-блокаторы, назначаемые после инфаркта миокарда при нормальной функции сердца, не приносят реальной пользы пациентам. Более того, у женщин, принимающих эти препараты, наблюдается повышенный риск смерти, повторных инфарктов и госпитализаций по поводу сердечной недостаточности.
a1a5e38df28d.original_description: A massive international study could upend 40 years of heart attack treatment. Researchers found that beta blockers—routinely prescribed after uncomplicated heart attacks—offered no real benefit for patients whose heart function remained normal, despite being given to millions worldwide. Even more surprising, women taking the drugs faced a higher risk of death, repeat heart attack, or hospitalization for heart failure compared to women who didn’t receive them.

eba95bf4a5d3.title: Surprising research reveals why you shouldn't add bananas to your smoothies
eba95bf4a5d3.summary: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body’s ability to absorb healthy flavanols. The surprising discovery shows that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.
eba95bf4a5d3.original_description: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body’s ability to absorb healthy flavanols. The surprising discovery shows that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.

1bb7f35b22b7.title: Scientists discover hidden driver of aging — Simple supplement reversed brain decline
1bb7f35b22b7.summary: Ученые обнаружили, что снижение уровня белка менин в гипоталамусе играет ключевую роль в процессах старения, вызывая воспаление и когнитивные нарушения у мышей. Восстановление уровня менина и прием добавки D-серина, аминокислоты, улучшали память и другие признаки старения. Это открытие открывает новые перспективы в борьбе с возрастными изменениями.
1bb7f35b22b7.original_description: A newly identified brain protein may play a major role in how the body ages. Researchers discovered that declining levels of Menin in the hypothalamus triggered inflammation, memory problems, bone loss, and other aging-related changes in mice. Restoring Menin reversed several of these effects, while a simple amino acid supplement called D-serine boosted cognition. The discovery opens a surprising new path for fighting age-related decline.

92eda5ae3e4f.title: Scientists “recharge” damaged nerves to ease chronic pain
92eda5ae3e4f.summary: For millions battling chronic nerve pain, even the softest touch can feel agonizing — but scientists may have uncovered a radically new way to stop it at the source. Researchers at Duke University found that damaged nerves can be revived by supplying them with healthy mitochondria, the tiny energy producers inside cells.
92eda5ae3e4f.original_description: For millions battling chronic nerve pain, even the softest touch can feel agonizing — but scientists may have uncovered a radically new way to stop it at the source. Researchers at Duke University found that damaged nerves can be revived by supplying them with healthy mitochondria, the tiny energy producers inside cells.

639481fd248d.title: Scientists discover hidden liver switch that cuts harmful cholesterol
639481fd248d.summary: Scientists at UT Southwestern have uncovered a surprising new “master switch” that helps control how much cholesterol the liver sends into the bloodstream. The newly identified protein, HELZ2, works by shutting down the genetic instructions needed to produce apoB — a key building block of the cholesterol-carrying particles linked to clogged arteries and heart disease.

6c514f2eef82.title: Scientists say house cats could help unlock new cancer treatments for humans
6c514f2eef82.summary: Scientists have cracked open the “black box” of feline cancer in a landmark study that genetically analyzed nearly 500 cat tumors from around the world. The research uncovered striking similarities between cancers in cats, dogs, and humans — including shared cancer-driving genes tied to aggressive breast cancers.

24d9b1160946.title: Scientists discover the oldest wooden tools ever used by humans
24d9b1160946.summary: Scientists have uncovered the oldest known hand-held wooden tools ever used by humans — and they’re an astonishing 430,000 years old. Buried for hundreds of thousands of years at an ancient lakeside site in Greece, the carefully carved wooden objects reveal that early humans were far more skilled and resourceful than once believed.

97193a617c24.title: AI scans 400,000 Reddit posts and finds hidden Ozempic side effects
97193a617c24.summary: Искусственный интеллект проанализировал более 400 000 сообщений на Reddit и выявил скрытые побочные эффекты у пользователей препаратов для снижения веса на основе GLP-1, такие как нарушения менструального цикла, озноб и приливы жара. Это исследование показывает потенциал использования социальных сетей для раннего обнаружения побочных эффектов, которые могут не выявляться в клинических испытаниях.
97193a617c24.original_description: By analyzing over 400,000 Reddit posts, researchers discovered that users of popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs frequently discussed unexpected symptoms like menstrual irregularities, chills, and hot flashes. The findings suggest AI could turn social media into a powerful early-warning system for spotting side effects that clinical trials may miss.

8d22be5b9cd2.title: Scientists may have found the source of the most powerful neutrino ever detected
8d22be5b9cd2.summary: Ученые предположительно установили источник самого мощного когда-либо зарегистрированного нейтрино. Исследователи считают, что причиной такого события могли стать блазары – сверхмассивные черные дыры, выбрасывающие потоки материи в сторону Земли.
8d22be5b9cd2.original_description: A mysterious particle from deep space has scientists buzzing after the most energetic neutrino ever detected slammed through the Mediterranean Sea. Now, researchers think they may have identified the cosmic “culprits” behind it: blazars — supermassive black holes blasting jets of matter straight toward Earth.

27f8214b2d1e.title: Scientists supercharge natural killer cells to fight aggressive cancers
27f8214b2d1e.summary: Scientists at McGill University have found a way to supercharge the immune system’s natural killer (NK) cells, helping them break through the defenses tumors use to stay alive. By temporarily blocking two proteins, researchers turned these cells into far more effective cancer fighters against difficult cancers like leukemia, glioblastoma, kidney cancer, and triple-negative breast cancer.

57859a71d55b.title: Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cells refuse to die
57859a71d55b.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising twist in how cells behave when division goes wrong. Sometimes a cell successfully copies its DNA but fails to split into two, leaving it with double the genetic material — a mistake linked to aging, cancer, and other major diseases. Researchers discovered that not all of these failures are equal.

706efb1d8793.title: Scientists discover atoms suddenly spinning backward in quantum experiment
706efb1d8793.summary: Scientists have directly watched angular momentum move through a crystal for the very first time — and discovered a bizarre twist along the way. Using ultra-powerful terahertz laser pulses, researchers triggered tiny atomic rotations inside a quantum material and found that the direction of rotation can unexpectedly flip as momentum is transferred. The strange reversal happens because of the crystal’s underlying symmetry, creating an almost impossible-sounding effect where two rotations combine

4794425f048a.title: Scientists warn popular vitamin D supplement may have a “previously unknown” downside
4794425f048a.summary: Новое исследование предполагает, что популярные добавки с витамином D2 могут снижать уровень более эффективной формы витамина D3 в организме. Ученые обнаружили, что D3 не только лучше повышает уровень витамина D, но и играет важную роль в укреплении иммунитета. Это открытие заставляет пересмотреть вопрос о предпочтительности D3 для приема в качестве добавки.
4794425f048a.original_description: A surprising study suggests vitamin D2 supplements may reduce the body’s levels of vitamin D3 — the more effective form of vitamin D. Researchers found D3 not only boosts vitamin D status more efficiently, but may also play a unique role in helping the immune system fight off viruses and bacteria. The discovery is prompting scientists to rethink whether D3 should become the preferred choice for supplementation.

a45d0ecec44a.title: Scientists discover simple way to relieve arthritis pain without pills or surgery
a45d0ecec44a.summary: A surprisingly simple walking tweak may offer new hope for millions living with knee osteoarthritis. In a year-long clinical trial, researchers found that slightly changing the angle of a person’s foot while walking reduced knee pain as effectively as common medications — and even slowed cartilage damage inside the joint.
